section.title: Chief Complaint
section.text: 80-year-old female presenting with 3-day history of fever (103.2°F), productive cough with yellow-green sputum, progressive shortness of breath, and decreased oxygen saturation to 88% on room air at home. Accompanied by daughter who reports decreased oral intake and increased confusion over the past 24 hours.

section.title: Hospital Course
section.text: Patient admitted to the medical floor on supplemental oxygen. Chest X-ray revealed left lower lobe infiltrate consistent with pneumonia. Blood cultures and sputum cultures obtained. Initiated on IV ceftriaxone and azithromycin per community-acquired pneumonia protocol. Urine Legionella and pneumococcal antigens negative. Blood cultures showed no growth at 5 days. Sputum culture grew Streptococcus pneumoniae, pan-sensitive. Delirium evaluation performed; likely secondary to infection. Patient improved clinically over 4 days with resolution of fever and improvement in oxygenation to 95% on room air. Transitioned to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate for discharge. Geriatrics consulted; recommended outpatient cognitive evaluation.

section.title: Discharge Diagnoses
section.text: 1. Community-acquired pneumonia, left lower lobe — Streptococcus pneumoniae (J18.9) — primary 2. Acute hypoxic respiratory failure (J96.01) 3. Delirium, secondary to infection (F05) 4. Chronic atrial fibrillation (I48.20) 5. Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (I50.30)

section.title: Discharge Medications
section.text: NEW: 1. Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg twice daily x 5 days (to complete 7-day course) CONTINUED: 2. Apixaban 5 mg twice daily (dose-reduced to 2.5 mg twice daily per age/weight criteria) 3. Metoprolol succinate 25 mg daily 4. Furosemide 20 mg daily 5. Lisinopril 5 mg daily (held while creatinine elevated — recheck in 5 days)
